 {"id":"bd-29lr","title":"Phase 1: Foundation - fastapi-output Crate and Core Infrastructure","description":"# Phase 1: Foundation

## Purpose

Establish the core infrastructure for rich output integration by creating the new fastapi-output 
crate with agent detection, mode switching, and the RichOutput facade API.

## Why This Phase First

Everything else depends on this foundation:
- Agent detection determines which mode to use
- OutputMode enum is used by all components
- RichOutput facade is the unified API all other phases call
- Theme system defines colors used everywhere

Without this phase, no other rich output work can proceed.

## Deliverables

1. **New crate: fastapi-output** (crates/fastapi-output/)
   - Cargo.toml with rich_rust as optional dependency
   - Module structure for all subcomponents
   - Re-exports for convenient API access

2. **Agent Detection System** (detection.rs)
   - is_agent_environment() function
   - Environment variable checks (CLAUDE_CODE, CODEX_CLI, etc.)
   - TTY detection via crossterm
   - NO_COLOR standard support
   - FORCE_COLOR override

3. **OutputMode System** (mode.rs)
   - OutputMode enum (Rich, Plain, Minimal)
   - Mode selection logic
   - Runtime mode switching support

4. **RichOutput Facade** (facade.rs)
   - Central RichOutput struct
   - Mode-aware method dispatching
   - Global instance management (lazy static)
   - Builder pattern for configuration

5. **Theme System** (themes.rs)
   - FastApiTheme struct with all color definitions
   - ThemePreset enum for built-in themes
   - Color constants for HTTP methods, status codes
   - Theme loading and application

## Technical Decisions

### Why a Separate Crate?

1. **Modularity**: Can be disabled entirely via feature flag
2. **Compile Time**: Only compiled when needed
3. **Dependency Isolation**: rich_rust dependency contained
4. **Testing**: Can test output logic independently

### Why Optional rich_rust Dependency?

When the "rich" feature is disabled:
- No rich_rust code compiled
- Plain mode is the only mode
- Minimal binary size impact
- Zero runtime overhead

### Global Instance vs Passed Reference?

Using a global lazy_static instance because:
- Output happens everywhere in the codebase
- Passing RichOutput through every function is impractical
- One-time initialization is fine for this use case
- Thread-safe via LazyLock

## Files to Create

```
crates/fastapi-output/
├── Cargo.toml
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs           # Public API, prelude
│   ├── detection.rs     # Agent environment detection
│   ├── mode.rs          # OutputMode enum and logic
│   ├── facade.rs        # RichOutput struct
│   └── themes.rs        # Theme definitions
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `cargo build -p fastapi-output` succeeds
2. `cargo build -p fastapi-output --no-default-features` succeeds (plain mode only)
3. Agent detection correctly identifies Claude Code, Codex, etc.
4. Mode switching works at runtime
5. Theme colors render correctly in rich mode
6. Unit tests pass for all detection heuristics","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:02:53.810604677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:44:13.642586798Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:44:13.642507057Z","close_reason":"Foundation checks done; output crate builds with and without rich; FORCE_COLOR override added","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","phase-1","rich-output"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29lr","depends_on_id":"bd-nww8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:25:13Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2buz","title":"Implement OpenAPI endpoint table display","description":"## Task: OpenAPI Endpoint Table Display

### Parent Feature
Phase 5: OpenAPI Display & Visual Polish (bd-urty)

### Overview
Create a visual table display of all OpenAPI-documented endpoints, showing methods, paths, summaries, and tags in a scannable format.

### Background & Reasoning
When developing APIs, having a quick overview of all endpoints helps:
- Verify all routes are documented
- Check for naming consistency
- Understand API scope at a glance
- Debug missing endpoints

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structures
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/openapi_display.rs

pub struct OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
    pub endpoints: Vec<EndpointInfo>,
    pub group_by_tag: bool,
    pub show_responses: bool,
}

pub struct EndpointInfo {
    pub method: HttpMethod,
    pub path: String,
    pub operation_id: Option<String>,
    pub summary: Option<String>,
    pub tags: Vec<String>,
    pub deprecated: bool,
    pub auth_required: bool,
    pub response_codes: Vec<u16>,
}
```

#### Visualization

**Rich Mode**:
```
┌──────────────────────── API Endpoints ────────────────────────┐
│                                                                │
│ 12 endpoints documented                                        │
│                                                                │
│ Users                                                          │
│ ┌────────┬─────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐│
│ │ GET    │ /users              │ List all users               ││
│ │ POST   │ /users              │ Create a new user     🔒     ││
│ │ GET    │ /users/{id}         │ Get user by ID               ││
│ │ PUT    │ /users/{id}         │ Update user           🔒     ││
│ │ DELETE │ /users/{id}         │ Delete user           🔒     ││
│ └────────┴─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘│
│                                                                │
│ Items                                                          │
│ ┌────────┬─────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐│
│ │ GET    │ /items              │ List all items               ││
│ │ POST   │ /items              │ Create an item        🔒     ││
│ │ GET    │ /items/{id}         │ Get item by ID               ││
│ └────────┴─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘│
│                                                                │
│ 🔒 = Authentication required                                   │
│ ⚠ = Deprecated                                                │
│                                                                │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Plain Mode**:
```
API Endpoints (12 total):

[Users]
  GET    /users             List all users
  POST   /users             Create a new user [auth]
  GET    /users/{id}        Get user by ID
  PUT    /users/{id}        Update user [auth]
  DELETE /users/{id}        Delete user [auth]

[Items]
  GET    /items             List all items
  POST   /items             Create an item [auth]
  GET    /items/{id}        Get item by ID
```

### Implementation
```rust
impl OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        let mut content = Vec::new();
        
        content.push(String::new());
        content.push(format!("  {} endpoints documented", self.endpoints.len()));
        content.push(String::new());
        
        // Group by tag if enabled
        let groups = if self.group_by_tag {
            self.group_endpoints_by_tag()
        } else {
            vec![("All".to_string(), self.endpoints.clone())]
        };
        
        for (tag, endpoints) in groups {
            if self.group_by_tag {
                content.push(theme.style_text(&format!("  {}", tag), "bold"));
            }
            
            let mut table = Table::new()
                .with_column(Column::new("").width(8))
                .with_column(Column::new("").width(25))
                .with_column(Column::new("").max_width(40));
            
            for ep in &endpoints {
                let method_styled = theme.style_method(&ep.method);
                
                let mut summary = ep.summary.clone().unwrap_or_default();
                
                // Add indicators
                let mut indicators = Vec::new();
                if ep.auth_required {
                    indicators.push("🔒");
                }
                if ep.deprecated {
                    indicators.push("⚠");
                }
                
                if !indicators.is_empty() {
                    summary = format!("{} {}", summary, indicators.join(" "));
                }
                
                table.add_row_cells([
                    method_styled,
                    ep.path.clone(),
                    summary,
                ]);
            }
            
            content.push(format!("{}", table.render_to_string()));
            content.push(String::new());
        }
        
        // Legend
        content.push(theme.style_text("  🔒 = Authentication required", "dim"));
        content.push(theme.style_text("  ⚠ = Deprecated", "dim"));
        content.push(String::new());
        
        let panel = Panel::from_text(&content.join("\n"))
            .title("API Endpoints")
            .rounded();
        
        output.renderable(&panel);
    }
}
```

### Integration with OpenAPI Crate
```rust
// In fastapi-openapi/src/lib.rs

impl OpenApiSpec {
    pub fn display_endpoints(&self) {
        let endpoints: Vec<EndpointInfo> = self.paths.iter()
            .flat_map(|(path, ops)| {
                ops.iter().map(|(method, op)| EndpointInfo {
                    method: method.clone(),
                    path: path.clone(),
                    operation_id: op.operation_id.clone(),
                    summary: op.summary.clone(),
                    tags: op.tags.clone(),
                    deprecated: op.deprecated,
                    auth_required: !op.security.is_empty(),
                    response_codes: op.responses.keys().cloned().collect(),
                })
            })
            .collect();
        
        OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
            endpoints,
            group_by_tag: true,
            show_responses: false,
        }.display();
    }
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EndpointInfo data structure
- [ ] Table display with method coloring
- [ ] Tag-based grouping (optional)
- [ ] Auth requirement indicator (🔒)
- [ ] Deprecation indicator (⚠)
- [ ] Endpoint count summary
- [ ] Legend for indicators
- [ ] Plain text fallback
- [ ] Integration with OpenAPI spec
- [ ] Unit tests

### Dependencies
- bd-36uw (RichOutput facade)
- bd-14v4 (FastApiTheme)
- bd-urty (Phase 5 feature)

+{"id":"bd-2c9t","title":"HTTP/2: server + framing + HPACK + tests (asupersync-only)","description":"Implement HTTP/2 support without Tokio/Hyper: connection preface, streams, framing, flow control, HPACK header compression, and parity-focused tests.","notes":"2026-02-10 (ChartreuseAnchor):
- Starting bd-2c9t per bv --robot-triage top pick.
- Plan: implement HTTP/2 cleartext (h2c prior knowledge first) in fastapi-http: frame codec + HPACK decoder (incl Huffman) + basic connection state (SETTINGS, stream lifecycle, flow control), then map streams to existing Request/Response and add end-to-end tests using asupersync only.
- First milestone: parse preface + SETTINGS exchange + decode HEADERS for a simple GET with non-huffman literals; respond with minimal headers + DATA.


2026-02-10 (WildCave):
- Implemented HTTP/2 h2c prior-knowledge sniffing + server integration (App + Handler + generic process_connection).
- Added HTTP/2 response HPACK encoder (literal without indexing, no huffman) and HEADERS/DATA framing (incl CONTINUATION split, streamed bodies).
- Added E2E TCP test: HTTP/2 preface + SETTINGS + HEADERS GET / (RFC 7541 C.2.1) -> :status 200 + body.
- Quality gates: cargo fmt/check/clippy/test all clean.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:34:52.947463023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:52:41.366475824Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2c9t","depends_on_id":"bd-uz2s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:34:52.953878405Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
